---
name: Agent-ready implementation
about: A scoped issue that is ready for deterministic implementation by a developer or agent
title: ""
labels: []
assignees: []
---

## Summary

Describe the change in a few short paragraphs.

Include:

- what should change
- why it should change
- the outcome this issue is meant to produce

## Execution Readiness

- [ ] This issue is ready for deterministic implementation
- [ ] This issue still needs further clarification or design work

If this issue is not ready for deterministic implementation, convert it into a discovery/shaping issue or clearly document the remaining open questions below.

## Desired Behaviour

Describe the intended behaviour or end state as precisely as possible.

Focus on externally observable behaviour or clearly defined internal outcomes.

## Scope

Describe what is in scope for this issue.

Be explicit about the area of the codebase, feature, workflow, or behaviour this issue is allowed to touch.

## Non-goals

List what this issue is not intended to do.

Use this section to prevent scope creep and unnecessary interpretation.

## Acceptance Criteria

List the criteria that determine whether this issue is complete.

Examples:

- [ ] Behaviour X occurs under condition Y
- [ ] Existing behaviour Z remains unchanged
- [ ] Validation command A passes
- [ ] Documentation B is updated

## Constraints and Requirements

List any constraints that must be respected.

Examples:

- architectural constraints
- domain rules
- performance requirements
- security or permissions requirements
- backward compatibility requirements
- UI or content constraints
- dependency constraints

## Relevant Context

Link any relevant context here.

Examples:

- related issues
- PRs
- docs
- architecture notes
- screenshots
- example files
- logs
- incident reports

## Validation Expectations

Describe how the work should be validated.

Examples:

- unit tests
- integration tests
- end-to-end tests
- manual verification steps
- linting / type checks / coverage checks
- specific CI expectations

## Open Questions

List any remaining questions.

If this section is non-empty in a way that materially affects implementation, the issue is probably not yet ready for deterministic execution.

## Notes for the Implementer

Add any extra details that would help a developer or agent complete the task consistently.
